title: Vite + React: first application
description: Build a small storefront-style app in incremental GitCourse lessons.

sections.title: Project setup
sections.description: Validate the starter toolchain and basic app structure.

lessons.title: Clone and run
lessons.objective: Install dependencies and boot the app locally.
lessons.ask_context: The student is preparing the Vite + React repository and validating the local toolchain.
hints: Use npm run build to validate CI readiness.

lessons.title: Create the header
lessons.objective: Add a reusable Header component with navigation links.
lessons.ask_context: The student is creating the first presentational React component.

lessons.title: Add routing
lessons.objective: Prepare Home, About, and Catalog pages with react-router.
lessons.ask_context: The student is introducing client-side routing into the starter app.
hints: Install react-router-dom when you are ready.

sections.title: Catalog experience
sections.description: Build a small catalog view with data and styles.

lessons.title: Catalog page with mock data
lessons.objective: Render a list of products or course cards from mock data.
lessons.ask_context: The student is building the first list page and shaping mock data for rendering.
hints: Keep the mock data local first.

lessons.title: Fetch JSON data
lessons.objective: Load data from a local JSON source or stub API.
lessons.ask_context: The student is replacing inline mock data with a fetch-based data source.
hints: Use useEffect for the first fetch.

lessons.title: Tailwind styling
lessons.objective: Apply responsive layout and dark mode friendly UI.
lessons.ask_context: The student is improving the layout and visual hierarchy with Tailwind.
hints: Use grid utilities for the catalog.

checklist.label: Tailwind utility classes were added to the app

lessons.title: Production build
lessons.objective: Prepare the app for release and preview the result.
lessons.ask_context: The student is validating the final production build and preview flow.
hints: The build should stay green after all previous changes.
Use npm run preview to inspect the final bundle.
